pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 14 defines PROJECT_DIR variable with exact path

setup_task.sh creates resource directories values/ (en), values-fr/ (fr), values-es/ (es) at lines 18-19. ISO 639-1 standard confirms en=English, fr=French, es=Spanish (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639_language_codes)

Standard Android project structure for locale configuration files. Verified against Android Developer documentation (https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/resources/app-languages) which specifies files should be placed in app/src/main/res/xml/

pi_items.metadata_value: AppCompatDelegate.setApplicationLocales() is a valid Android API for locale switching
pi_items.verified_value: AppCompatDelegate.setApplicationLocales() is a real Android API from androidx.appcompat library
pi_items.source: Android Developers official documentation (https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/resources/app-languages) confirms this API exists and documents its usage for per-app language preferences

pi_items.metadata_value: locales_config.xml is a valid Android configuration file for defining supported locales
pi_items.verified_value: locales_config.xml (also called locale-config.xml) is a real Android configuration file introduced in Android 13
pi_items.source: Android Developers official documentation (https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/resources/app-languages) and Google Android Developers Blog (https://android-developers.googleblog.com/2022/11/per-app-language-preferences-part-1.html) confirm this file enables per-app language preferences

pi_items.metadata_value: AndroidManifest.xml uses android:localeConfig attribute to register locale configuration
pi_items.verified_value: android:localeConfig attribute exists in AndroidManifest.xml for registering locale configuration
pi_items.source: Android Developers official documentation (https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/resources/app-languages) specifies using android:localeConfig attribute in the application tag to reference locale-config.xml

pi_items.metadata_value: MainActivity should extend AppCompatActivity for proper locale switching
pi_items.verified_value: AppCompatActivity is the correct base class for using AppCompatDelegate.setApplicationLocales()
pi_items.source: Android Developers documentation (https://developer.android.com/reference/androidx/appcompat/app/AppCompatActivity) confirms AppCompatActivity is required for AppCompat features

pi_items.metadata_value: LocaleListCompat is used to create locale objects for setApplicationLocales()
pi_items.verified_value: LocaleListCompat is a real Android class for creating locale lists with setApplicationLocales()
pi_items.source: Android Developers documentation (https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/resources/app-languages) provides example: LocaleListCompat.forLanguageTags("xx-YY")

privileged_info_summary: This task uses a synthetic Android project created by setup_task.sh. The metadata accurately describes the project structure and file paths. The task implements per-app language preferences for a Kotlin Android app named 'PolyglotReader' by creating a locales_config.xml file, registering it in AndroidManifest.xml with the android:localeConfig attribute, and implementing language switching in MainActivity.kt using AppCompatDelegate.setApplicationLocales() with LocaleListCompat. Expected locales are English (en), French (fr), and Spanish (es), which are standard ISO 639-1 language codes. All Android APIs and configuration approaches referenced are verified as real, documented Android features available in androidx.appcompat library.
